papers.title: MosaicMem: Hybrid Spatial Memory for Controllable Video World Models
papers.abstract: Abstract Video diffusion models use hybrid spatial memory to maintain consistency under camera motion and enable long-term scene editing and navigation.  AI-generated summary Video diffusion models are moving beyond short, plausible clips toward world simulators that must remain consistent under camera motion, revisits, and intervention. Yet spatial memory remains a key bottleneck: explicit 3D structures can improve reprojection-based consistency but struggle to depict moving objects, while implicit memory often produces inaccurate camera motion even with correct poses. We propose Mosaic Memory (MosaicMem), a hybrid spatial memory that lifts patches into 3D for reliable localization and targeted retrieval, while exploiting the model's native conditioning to preserve prompt-following generation. MosaicMem composes spatially aligned patches in the queried view via a patch-and-compose interface, preserving what should persist while allowing the model to inpaint what should evolve. With PRoPE camera conditioning and two new memory alignment methods, experiments show improved pose adherence compared to implicit memory and stronger dynamic modeling than explicit baselines. MosaicMem further enables minute-level navigation, memory-based scene editing, and autoregressive rollout.

ru.title: Гибридная память для согласованной генерации видео при движении камеры
ru.desc: В работе представлена Mosaic Memory — гибридная система пространственной памяти для видеодиффузионных моделей, которая преодолевает ограничения чистых 3D-структур и неявного представления. Система поднимает пиксельные патчи в трёхмерное пространство для надёжной локализации и выборки, одновременно сохраняя способность генеративной модели следовать исходному промпту. Гибридный подход позволяет сохранять статичные части сцены с высокой точностью, в то время как динамические объекты генерируются заново в соответствии с движением камеры. Экспериментально показано, что метод обеспечивает лучшее следование позам камеры и моделирование динамики по сравнению с существующими альтернативами.

en.title: Mosaic Memory: Enhancing Video Consistency and Navigation
en.desc: This paper introduces Mosaic Memory (MosaicMem), a new approach to video diffusion models that enhances spatial memory for better consistency during camera motion. It combines explicit 3D structures with implicit memory to improve the accuracy of camera movements and dynamic object representation. MosaicMem allows for targeted retrieval and reliable localization by lifting patches into 3D, enabling effective scene editing and navigation. The proposed methods demonstrate significant improvements in pose adherence and dynamic modeling, making it a powerful tool for long-term video generation and manipulation.

zh.title: 混合空间记忆：提升视频生成的一致性与灵活性
zh.desc: 视频扩散模型利用混合空间记忆在相机运动下保持一致性，并实现长期场景编辑和导航。我们提出的Mosaic Memory（MosaicMem）通过将图像块提升到3D空间，提供可靠的定位和有针对性的检索，同时利用模型的原生条件生成来保持生成的连贯性。MosaicMem通过补丁和组合接口在查询视图中组合空间对齐的图像块，保留应持续存在的内容，同时允许模型对应变化的部分进行修补。实验表明，MosaicMem在姿态遵循和动态建模方面优于现有的隐式和显式记忆方法。

papers.title: MetaClaw: Just Talk -- An Agent That Meta-Learns and Evolves in the Wild
papers.abstract: Abstract A continual meta-learning framework for large language model agents that jointly evolves policies and reusable behavioral skills while minimizing downtime through opportunistic updates and skill-driven adaptation.  AI-generated summary Large language model (LLM) agents are increasingly used for complex tasks, yet deployed agents often remain static, failing to adapt as user needs evolve. This creates a tension between the need for continuous service and the necessity of updating capabilities to match shifting task distributions. On platforms like OpenClaw, which handle diverse workloads across 20+ channels, existing methods either store raw trajectories without distilling knowledge, maintain static skill libraries, or require disruptive downtime for retraining. We present MetaClaw, a continual meta-learning framework that jointly evolves a base LLM policy and a library of reusable behavioral skills. MetaClaw employs two complementary mechanisms. Skill-driven fast adaptation analyzes failure trajectories via an LLM evolver to synthesize new skills, enabling immediate improvement with zero downtime. Opportunistic policy optimization performs gradient-based updates via cloud LoRA fine-tuning and Reinforcement Learning with a Process Reward Model (RL-PRM). This is triggered during user-inactive windows by the Opportunistic Meta-Learning Scheduler (OMLS), which monitors system inactivity and calendar data. These mechanisms are mutually reinforcing: a refined policy generates better trajectories for skill synthesis, while richer skills provide higher-quality data for policy optimization. To prevent data contamination, a versioning mechanism separates support and query data. Built on a proxy-based architecture, MetaClaw scales to production-size LLMs without local GPUs. Experiments on MetaClaw-Bench and AutoResearchClaw show that skill-driven adaptation improves accuracy by up to 32% relative. The full pipeline advances Kimi-K2.5 accuracy from 21.4% to 40.6% and increases composite robustness by 18.3%. Code is available at https://github.com/aiming-lab/MetaClaw.

ru.title: Непрерывное обучение LLM-агентов через адаптивные навыки без простоев
ru.desc: MetaClaw — это фреймворк непрерывного метаобучения для LLM-агентов, который одновременно развивает базовую политику и библиотеку переиспользуемых поведенческих навыков. Система использует два механизма: адаптация на основе навыков анализирует неудачные траектории и синтезирует новые умения без простоев, а оптимизация политики выполняется через LoRA fine-tuning и обучение с подкреплением в неактивные окна пользователей. Версионирование данных предотвращает загрязнение информации между обучающей и тестовой выборками. Экспериментальные результаты показывают значительный прирост точности моделей на реальных задачах.

en.title: MetaClaw: Evolving LLMs with Zero Downtime
en.desc: This paper introduces MetaClaw, a continual meta-learning framework designed for large language model (LLM) agents to enhance their adaptability and performance. It focuses on evolving both the LLM policy and a library of reusable behavioral skills without causing downtime, which is crucial for maintaining continuous service. The framework utilizes skill-driven fast adaptation to quickly synthesize new skills from failure data and opportunistic policy optimization to refine the LLM during inactive periods. By integrating these mechanisms, MetaClaw significantly improves task accuracy and robustness while efficiently managing data contamination through a versioning system.

zh.title: 持续元学习，动态适应能力！
zh.desc: 本文提出了一种持续的元学习框架MetaClaw，旨在为大型语言模型代理提供动态适应能力。该框架通过技能驱动的快速适应和机会性策略优化，能够在用户不活跃时进行更新，避免了停机时间。MetaClaw结合了基于梯度的更新和强化学习，提升了模型的准确性和鲁棒性。实验结果表明，MetaClaw在多个任务上显著提高了性能，展示了其在复杂环境中的应用潜力。

papers.title: Stereo World Model: Camera-Guided Stereo Video Generation
papers.abstract: Abstract StereoWorld is a camera-conditioned stereo world model that generates stereo videos end-to-end using RGB modality while maintaining geometric consistency and efficiency through novel attention mechanisms.  AI-generated summary We present StereoWorld, a camera-conditioned stereo world model that jointly learns appearance and binocular geometry for end-to-end stereo video generation.Unlike monocular RGB or RGBD approaches, StereoWorld operates exclusively within the RGB modality, while simultaneously grounding geometry directly from disparity. To efficiently achieve consistent stereo generation, our approach introduces two key designs: (1) a unified camera-frame RoPE that augments latent tokens with camera-aware rotary positional encoding, enabling relative, view- and time-consistent conditioning while preserving pretrained video priors via a stable attention initialization; and (2) a stereo-aware attention decomposition that factors full 4D attention into 3D intra-view attention plus horizontal row attention, leveraging the epipolar prior to capture disparity-aligned correspondences with substantially lower compute. Across benchmarks, StereoWorld improves stereo consistency, disparity accuracy, and camera-motion fidelity over strong monocular-then-convert pipelines, achieving more than 3x faster generation with an additional 5% gain in viewpoint consistency. Beyond benchmarks, StereoWorld enables end-to-end binocular VR rendering without depth estimation or inpainting, enhances embodied policy learning through metric-scale depth grounding, and is compatible with long-video distillation for extended interactive stereo synthesis.

ru.title: Двумерное зрение: end-to-end генерация стереовидео с геометрической консистентностью
ru.desc: StereoWorld — это модель world model, которая генерирует стереовидео конца-в-конец, работая исключительно с RGB модальностью и одновременно обучаясь геометрии на основе диспаритета. Модель использует две ключевые архитектурные инновации: unified camera-frame RoPE для добавления позиционного кодирования с учётом камеры и стерео-aware attention decomposition, которая факторизует 4D внимание в 3D внутривидовое внимание и горизонтальное построчное внимание. Подход существенно превосходит конвейеры mono-to-stereo преобразования, обеспечивая более чем трёхкратное ускорение генерации и 5% улучшение консистентности точки зрения. StereoWorld открывает новые возможности для VR рендеринга, обучения воплощённых агентов и синтеза длинных интерактивных стереовидео.

en.title: Revolutionizing Stereo Video Generation with RGB-Only Models
en.desc: StereoWorld is a novel stereo video generation model that uses only RGB images while ensuring geometric accuracy. It learns both visual appearance and depth information together, which allows it to create stereo videos efficiently. The model introduces innovative techniques like camera-aware positional encoding and a specialized attention mechanism to improve performance and reduce computational costs. As a result, StereoWorld not only enhances the quality of generated videos but also speeds up the generation process significantly compared to traditional methods.

zh.title: StereoWorld：高效生成立体视频的创新模型
zh.desc: StereoWorld 是一种相机条件的立体世界模型，能够通过 RGB 模态生成立体视频，同时保持几何一致性和高效性。该模型联合学习外观和双目几何，专注于 RGB 模态，而直接从视差中获取几何信息。为了实现一致的立体生成，StereoWorld 引入了两个关键设计：统一的相机帧 RoPE 和立体感知注意力分解。通过这些创新，StereoWorld 在多个基准测试中提高了立体一致性和视差准确性，并且生成速度比传统方法快了三倍以上。

papers.title: When AI Navigates the Fog of War
papers.abstract: Abstract Large language models demonstrate varying capabilities in reasoning about unfolding geopolitical conflicts, showing strategic realism in structured settings but inconsistent performance in complex political environments.  AI-generated summary Can AI reason about a war before its trajectory becomes historically obvious? Analyzing this capability is difficult because retrospective geopolitical prediction is heavily confounded by training-data leakage. We address this challenge through a temporally grounded case study of the early stages of the 2026 Middle East conflict, which unfolded after the training cutoff of current frontier models. We construct 11 critical temporal nodes, 42 node-specific verifiable questions, and 5 general exploratory questions, requiring models to reason only from information that would have been publicly available at each moment. This design substantially mitigates training-data leakage concerns, creating a setting well-suited for studying how models analyze an unfolding crisis under the fog of war, and provides, to our knowledge, the first temporally grounded analysis of LLM reasoning in an ongoing geopolitical conflict. Our analysis reveals three main findings. First, current state-of-the-art large language models often display a striking degree of strategic realism, reasoning beyond surface rhetoric toward deeper structural incentives. Second, this capability is uneven across domains: models are more reliable in economically and logistically structured settings than in politically ambiguous multi-actor environments. Finally, model narratives evolve over time, shifting from early expectations of rapid containment toward more systemic accounts of regional entrenchment and attritional de-escalation. Since the conflict remains ongoing at the time of writing, this work can serve as an archival snapshot of model reasoning during an unfolding geopolitical crisis, enabling future studies without the hindsight bias of retrospective analysis.
affiliations: Mohamed bin Zayed University of Artificial Intelligence, Abu Dhabi, United Arab Emirates
University of Maryland, College Park, United States

ru.title: Могут ли нейросети предсказывать войну во мраке неопределённости?
ru.desc: В статье исследуется способность больших языковых моделей рассуждать о развивающихся геополитических конфликтах в реальном времени, используя конфликт на Ближнем Востоке 2026 года как тестовый случай. Авторы создали набор из 42 верифицируемых вопросов в 11 критических временных точках, основанных только на информации, доступной в каждый конкретный момент, что позволило избежать утечки данных из обучающего набора. Исследование показало, что современные LLM демонстрируют стратегический реализм в структурированных экономических и логистических сценариях, но менее надёжны в политически неоднозначных многоакторных окружениях. Работа представляет первый темпорально обоснованный анализ рассуждений LLM в развивающемся геополитическом конфликте, создавая архивный снимок когнитивных процессов моделей без ретроспективного смещения.

en.title: Unraveling LLMs: Reasoning in Real-Time Geopolitical Conflicts
en.desc: This paper investigates how large language models (LLMs) reason about geopolitical conflicts, particularly focusing on the early stages of the 2026 Middle East conflict. It introduces a novel approach by creating a temporally grounded case study that avoids training-data leakage, allowing for a clearer analysis of model reasoning in real-time scenarios. The findings indicate that while LLMs can demonstrate strategic realism, their performance varies significantly depending on the complexity of the political environment. Additionally, the models' narratives evolve as the situation develops, reflecting a shift from initial containment strategies to more complex understandings of the conflict's dynamics.

zh.title: 大型语言模型在地缘政治冲突中的推理能力
zh.desc: 这篇论文探讨了大型语言模型在分析地缘政治冲突时的推理能力。研究表明，这些模型在结构化环境中表现出战略现实主义，但在复杂的政治环境中表现不一致。通过对2026年中东冲突早期阶段的案例研究，作者构建了多个时间节点和可验证的问题，以减少训练数据泄漏的影响。研究发现，当前的语言模型在经济和后勤结构明确的环境中更可靠，而在政治模糊的多方环境中则表现较差。

papers.title: AdaMem: Adaptive User-Centric Memory for Long-Horizon Dialogue Agents
papers.abstract: Abstract AdaMem is an adaptive memory framework for dialogue agents that organizes conversation history into multiple memory types and uses conditional retrieval to improve long-horizon reasoning and user modeling.  AI-generated summary Large language model (LLM) agents increasingly rely on external memory to support long-horizon interaction, personalized assistance, and multi-step reasoning. However, existing memory systems still face three core challenges: they often rely too heavily on semantic similarity, which can miss evidence crucial for user-centric understanding; they frequently store related experiences as isolated fragments, weakening temporal and causal coherence; and they typically use static memory granularities that do not adapt well to the requirements of different questions. We propose AdaMem, an adaptive user-centric memory framework for long-horizon dialogue agents. AdaMem organizes dialogue history into working, episodic, persona, and graph memories, enabling the system to preserve recent context, structured long-term experiences, stable user traits, and relation-aware connections within a unified framework. At inference time, AdaMem first resolves the target participant, then builds a question-conditioned retrieval route that combines semantic retrieval with relation-aware graph expansion only when needed, and finally produces the answer through a role-specialized pipeline for evidence synthesis and response generation. We evaluate AdaMem on the LoCoMo and PERSONAMEM benchmarks for long-horizon reasoning and user modeling. Experimental results show that AdaMem achieves state-of-the-art performance on both benchmarks. The code will be released upon acceptance.

ru.title: Адаптивная память для глубокого понимания пользователя в долгих диалогах
ru.desc: AdaMem — это адаптивная система памяти для диалоговых агентов на основе больших языковых моделей, которая организует историю разговора в несколько типов памяти: рабочую, эпизодическую, персональную и графовую. Система использует условное извлечение информации, комбинируя семантический поиск с графовым расширением на основе отношений только при необходимости. Такой подход позволяет сохранить контекстуальность, причинно-следственные связи и адаптивную детализацию при ответах на разные вопросы. Экспериментальные результаты на бенчмарках LoCoMo и PERSONAMEM показывают, что AdaMem достигает наилучшей производительности в задачах долгосрочного рассуждения и моделирования пользователя.

en.title: AdaMem: Revolutionizing Memory for Dialogue Agents
en.desc: AdaMem is a novel memory framework designed for dialogue agents that enhances their ability to manage conversation history effectively. It categorizes memories into different types, such as working, episodic, persona, and graph memories, which helps in maintaining context and understanding user traits over time. The framework addresses key challenges in memory systems by using conditional retrieval that combines semantic understanding with relational context, improving the coherence of responses. AdaMem has demonstrated superior performance in long-horizon reasoning and user modeling on established benchmarks, showcasing its potential for advanced dialogue interactions.

zh.title: AdaMem：对话代理的适应性记忆框架
zh.desc: AdaMem是一种适应性记忆框架，专为对话代理设计，能够将对话历史组织成多种记忆类型，并通过条件检索来提升长时间推理和用户建模的能力。现有的记忆系统面临三个主要挑战，包括过度依赖语义相似性、存储相关经验为孤立片段以及静态记忆粒度不适应不同问题的需求。AdaMem通过工作记忆、情节记忆、个性记忆和图形记忆的组织方式，保持最近的上下文、结构化的长期经验、稳定的用户特征和关系感知的连接。实验结果表明，AdaMem在长时间推理和用户建模的基准测试中表现出色，达到了最先进的性能。

papers.title: Unified Spatio-Temporal Token Scoring for Efficient Video VLMs
papers.abstract: Abstract STTS is a lightweight module that efficiently prunes vision tokens across both vision transformer and language model architectures in vision-language models for video tasks, achieving significant computational gains with minimal performance loss.  AI-generated summary Token pruning is essential for enhancing the computational efficiency of vision-language models (VLMs), particularly for video-based tasks where temporal redundancy is prevalent. Prior approaches typically prune tokens either (1) within the vision transformer (ViT) exclusively for unimodal perception tasks such as action recognition and object segmentation, without adapting to downstream vision-language tasks; or (2) only within the LLM while leaving the ViT output intact, often requiring complex text-conditioned token selection mechanisms. In this paper, we introduce Spatio-Temporal Token Scoring (STTS), a simple and lightweight module that prunes vision tokens across both the ViT and the LLM without text conditioning or token merging, and is fully compatible with end-to-end training. By learning how to score temporally via an auxiliary loss and spatially via LLM downstream gradients, aided by our efficient packing algorithm, STTS prunes 50% of vision tokens throughout the entire architecture, resulting in a 62% improvement in efficiency during both training and inference with only a 0.7% drop in average performance across 13 short and long video QA tasks. Efficiency gains increase with more sampled frames per video. Applying test-time scaling for long-video QA further yields performance gains of 0.5-1% compared to the baseline. Overall, STTS represents a novel, simple yet effective technique for unified, architecture-wide vision token pruning.

ru.title: Умное удаление лишних визуальных токенов в видео-моделях
ru.desc: В статье предложен модуль STTS для сокращения избыточных визуальных токенов в видео-моделях, которые объединяют vision transformer и языковую модель. Метод использует вспомогательную функцию потерь для обучения временному скорингу токенов и градиенты языковой модели для пространственной фильтрации. Алгоритм позволяет удалить 50% визуальных токенов во всей архитектуре, улучшив эффективность на 62% при тренировке и инференсе с минимальным падением качества на 0.7%. Подход показывает универсальность и простоту в применении к различным задачам видео-вопросно-ответного диалога.

en.title: Efficient Token Pruning for Enhanced Video Task Performance
en.desc: The paper presents Spatio-Temporal Token Scoring (STTS), a new method for pruning vision tokens in vision-language models (VLMs) used for video tasks. STTS operates across both vision transformers and language models, enhancing computational efficiency by reducing the number of tokens processed without significant performance loss. Unlike previous methods that focused on unimodal tasks or required complex mechanisms, STTS simplifies the process by scoring tokens based on temporal and spatial information. The results show a 62% improvement in efficiency with only a minor drop in performance, demonstrating STTS's effectiveness in optimizing VLMs for video analysis.

zh.title: STTS：高效的视觉标记修剪技术
zh.desc: STTS是一种轻量级模块，能够有效地在视觉变换器和语言模型架构中修剪视觉标记，从而提高视频任务的计算效率。该方法通过学习时序评分和空间评分，能够在不依赖文本条件或标记合并的情况下，修剪50%的视觉标记。实验表明，STTS在训练和推理过程中实现了62%的效率提升，且平均性能仅下降0.7%。这种方法适用于端到端训练，具有广泛的应用潜力。

papers.title: LoST: Level of Semantics Tokenization for 3D Shapes
papers.abstract: Abstract Level-of-Semantics Tokenization (LoST) improves 3D shape generation by ordering tokens based on semantic salience and using a novel relational alignment loss for better reconstruction and efficiency.  AI-generated summary Tokenization is a fundamental technique in the generative modeling of various modalities. In particular, it plays a critical role in autoregressive (AR) models, which have recently emerged as a compelling option for 3D generation. However, optimal tokenization of 3D shapes remains an open question. State-of-the-art (SOTA) methods primarily rely on geometric level-of-detail (LoD) hierarchies, originally designed for rendering and compression. These spatial hierarchies are often token-inefficient and lack semantic coherence for AR modeling. We propose Level-of-Semantics Tokenization (LoST), which orders tokens by semantic salience, such that early prefixes decode into complete, plausible shapes that possess principal semantics, while subsequent tokens refine instance-specific geometric and semantic details. To train LoST, we introduce Relational Inter-Distance Alignment (RIDA), a novel 3D semantic alignment loss that aligns the relational structure of the 3D shape latent space with that of the semantic DINO feature space. Experiments show that LoST achieves SOTA reconstruction, surpassing previous LoD-based 3D shape tokenizers by large margins on both geometric and semantic reconstruction metrics. Moreover, LoST achieves efficient, high-quality AR 3D generation and enables downstream tasks like semantic retrieval, while using only 0.1%-10% of the tokens needed by prior AR models.

ru.title: Семантическая токенизация для эффективной генерации трёхмерных форм
ru.desc: В статье предложен новый метод токенизации трёхмерных форм под названием LoST, который упорядочивает токены по семантической значимости, а не по геометрическим уровням детализации. Авторы вводят специальную функцию потерь RIDA для выравнивания структуры латентного пространства 3D-форм с семантическим пространством признаков DINO. Метод позволяет генеративным моделям сначала создавать полные и правдоподобные формы с основными семантическими чертами, а затем последовательно добавлять геометрические детали. Экспериментальные результаты показывают, что LoST значительно превосходит существующие методы по качеству реконструкции и эффективности, используя при этом в 10-100 раз меньше токенов.

en.title: Revolutionizing 3D Shape Generation with Semantic Tokenization
en.desc: The paper introduces Level-of-Semantics Tokenization (LoST), a method that enhances 3D shape generation by organizing tokens based on their semantic importance. This approach allows for the initial tokens to create complete and plausible shapes, while later tokens add specific geometric and semantic details. To optimize this process, the authors propose a new loss function called Relational Inter-Distance Alignment (RIDA), which aligns the latent space of 3D shapes with semantic features. The results demonstrate that LoST significantly outperforms existing methods in both geometric and semantic reconstruction, while also being more efficient in token usage for autoregressive models.

zh.title: 语义驱动的3D形状生成新方法
zh.desc: 本论文提出了一种新的3D形状生成方法，称为语义层次标记化（LoST），通过根据语义重要性对标记进行排序来提高生成效果。LoST使用了一种新颖的关系对齐损失（RIDA），以更好地重建和提高效率。与传统的几何细节层次方法相比，LoST在几何和语义重建指标上都取得了显著的进展。实验结果表明，LoST在生成高质量的3D形状时，仅需使用之前自回归模型的0.1%-10%的标记。

papers.title: Complementary Reinforcement Learning
papers.abstract: Abstract Complementary RL enables efficient agent learning by synchronizing experience extraction with policy optimization through dual objectives that evolve together during training.  AI-generated summary Reinforcement Learning (RL) has emerged as a powerful paradigm for training LLM-based agents, yet remains limited by low sample efficiency, stemming not only from sparse outcome feedback but also from the agent's inability to leverage prior experience across episodes. While augmenting agents with historical experience offers a promising remedy, existing approaches suffer from a critical weakness: the experience distilled from history is either stored statically or fail to coevolve with the improving actor, causing a progressive misalignment between the experience and the actor's evolving capability that diminishes its utility over the course of training. Inspired by complementary learning systems in neuroscience, we present Complementary RL to achieve seamless co-evolution of an experience extractor and a policy actor within the RL optimization loop. Specifically, the actor is optimized via sparse outcome-based rewards, while the experience extractor is optimized according to whether its distilled experiences demonstrably contribute to the actor's success, thereby evolving its experience management strategy in lockstep with the actor's growing capabilities. Empirically, Complementary RL outperforms outcome-based agentic RL baselines that do not learn from experience, achieving 10% performance improvement in single-task scenarios and exhibits robust scalability in multi-task settings. These results establish Complementary RL as a paradigm for efficient experience-driven agent learning.

ru.title: Взаимодополняющее обучение: синхронизация опыта и политики
ru.desc: Статья представляет новый подход Complementary RL для обучения агентов с подкреплением, который одновременно оптимизирует политику и экстрактор опыта. Основная идея состоит в том, чтобы избежать рассогласования между накопленным опытом и развивающимися способностями агента путём их совместной эволюции во время тренировки. Экстрактор опыта обновляется на основе того, насколько его извлеченный опыт действительно способствует успеху политики, что обеспечивает синхронизацию обучения. Эмпирические результаты показывают 10% улучшение производительности в одноцелевых сценариях и хорошую масштабируемость в многоцелевых задачах.

en.title: Co-evolving Experience and Policy for Efficient Learning
en.desc: Complementary Reinforcement Learning (RL) introduces a novel approach to enhance agent learning by synchronizing the extraction of experiences with the optimization of the policy. This method addresses the common issue of low sample efficiency in RL, which arises from the agent's inability to effectively utilize past experiences. By allowing the experience extractor and the policy actor to evolve together, the framework ensures that the experiences remain relevant and beneficial as the agent improves. Empirical results demonstrate that Complementary RL significantly outperforms traditional RL methods, achieving better performance in both single-task and multi-task environments.

zh.title: 补充强化学习：高效的经验驱动智能体学习
zh.desc: 补充强化学习（Complementary RL）通过同步经验提取与策略优化，提升了智能体的学习效率。该方法解决了传统强化学习中样本效率低的问题，尤其是历史经验的利用不足。补充强化学习通过优化经验提取器和策略执行者，使得两者在训练过程中共同进化，从而提高了学习效果。实验结果表明，补充强化学习在单任务和多任务场景中均显著优于不利用经验的基线方法。

papers.title: LaDe: Unified Multi-Layered Graphic Media Generation and Decomposition
papers.abstract: Abstract LaDe is a latent diffusion framework that generates layered media designs with flexible layer counts and semantic meaning from natural language prompts, supporting text-to-image, text-to-layers, and media decomposition tasks.  AI-generated summary Media design layer generation enables the creation of fully editable, layered design documents such as posters, flyers, and logos using only natural language prompts. Existing methods either restrict outputs to a fixed number of layers or require each layer to contain only spatially continuous regions, causing the layer count to scale linearly with design complexity. We propose LaDe (Layered Media Design), a latent diffusion framework that generates a flexible number of semantically meaningful layers. LaDe combines three components: an LLM-based prompt expander that transforms a short user intent into structured per-layer descriptions that guide the generation, a Latent Diffusion Transformer with a 4D RoPE positional encoding mechanism that jointly generates the full media design and its constituent RGBA layers, and an RGBA VAE that decodes each layer with full alpha-channel support. By conditioning on layer samples during training, our unified framework supports three tasks: text-to-image generation, text-to-layers media design generation, and media design decomposition. We compare LaDe to Qwen-Image-Layered on text-to-layers and image-to-layers tasks on the Crello test set. LaDe outperforms Qwen-Image-Layered in text-to-layers generation by improving text-to-layer alignment, as validated by two VLM-as-a-judge evaluators (GPT-4o mini and Qwen3-VL).

ru.title: Многослойный дизайн из текста: диффузия с произвольным числом семантических слоёв
ru.desc: LaDe — это фреймворк латентной диффузии для генерации многослойных медиа-дизайнов с произвольным количеством семантически значимых слоёв из текстовых описаний. Система использует LLM для расширения промптов, трансформер диффузии с 4D позиционным кодированием RoPE и специальный RGBA VAE для декодирования слоёв с поддержкой альфа-канала. Единый фреймворк поддерживает три задачи: генерацию изображений по тексту, создание слоистых дизайнов и разложение готовых дизайнов на компоненты. Модель превосходит существующие методы по качеству выравнивания текста и слоёв при создании редактируемых документов для постеров, флаеров и логотипов.

en.title: Flexible Layered Media Design with LaDe
en.desc: LaDe is a novel framework that utilizes latent diffusion to create layered media designs from natural language prompts. It allows for a flexible number of layers, making it possible to generate complex designs like posters and logos without being limited by fixed layer counts. The framework includes a prompt expander that converts user intentions into detailed layer descriptions, a Latent Diffusion Transformer for generating the designs, and an RGBA VAE for decoding layers with alpha-channel support. LaDe has shown superior performance in generating text-to-layers compared to existing methods, enhancing the alignment between text prompts and generated layers.

zh.title: 灵活生成分层媒体设计的潜在扩散框架
zh.desc: LaDe是一种潜在扩散框架，可以根据自然语言提示生成具有灵活层数和语义意义的分层媒体设计。它支持文本到图像、文本到层和媒体分解等任务，能够创建完全可编辑的分层设计文档，如海报和标志。与现有方法不同，LaDe不限制输出层数，并且每层可以包含具有语义意义的内容。通过结合大语言模型和潜在扩散变换器，LaDe在文本到层生成任务中表现优于其他方法。
